SAPTA

SAPTA

Student Artists' Platform for Talent and Aspiration

About SAPTA

SAPTA is a non-profit, student-led organization that promotes Carnatic music. While providing students of all levels opportunities to perform, they also have a way to showcase their talents in unique ways. Proceeds of the funds will go to support underfunded areas in our Tri-Valley community.

Meet the Team

Ananya Aiyer

Ananya is a freshman at DVHS with a love for Carnatic music and a talent for bringing people together. She founded SAPTA to create a space where student musicians can perform, grow, and give back to the community.

Shravya Rengan

Shravya Rengan is a freshman at Emerald High school in Dublin. She has been learning Carnatic vocal and Carnatic Violin. Shravya is a dedicated volunteer who stays committed to meaningful service and positive change.

Upasana Arun

Upasana is a 7th grader at Windemere Ranch Middle School, San Ramon. She learns Carnatic vocal and Carnatic violin. She enjoys traveling, playing badminton, listening to music & lectures.

Krishnabandhu Srivathsa

Krishnabandhu Srivathsa is a ninth grader at Amador Valley High School, Pleasanton. He has been pursuing vocal music, Carnatic flute, and the Western flute. His other interests include flight simulation, PC building, and programming.

Anusha Naveen

Anusha Naveen is a seventh grader at Windemere Ranch Middle School who loves Carnatic music. She loves reading, musical theater, and singing/listening to pop music.
